title: Friday Sermon 1 January 1999
description:
Surely those who recite the Book of Allah and observe Prayer and spend out of what We have provided for them secretly and openly pursue a commerce that suffers no loss.

In order that He may give them their full rewards and add extra to them out of His bounty.  He is surely Most Forgiving, Most Appreciating.

(‘The Holy Quran’.  Al Fatir [Originator]: 30, 31).

It is common custom that greetings are offered for the New Year and gifts are also offered according to one’s capacity.  So on this occasion, I give a spiritual colour to this custom by offering the loving salutation to all friends: Peace be to you and the mercy of Allah and His blessings.

I offer this salutation with the prayer that the Almighty may make this year better than the previous year.  The troubles which were born last year, not only for the Ahmadiyya Muslim Community but for all humanity.  May He avert from us the trials and tribulations that we have experienced in the previous year.  May the troubles that were born last year, not be given the ability to gain any strength and be discarded before they yield any fruit and may the good deeds which were carried out last year gain abundant fruit and may they be a source of good results.

I have received reports from 75 countries on Waqf e Jadid. The actual collection this year is 1.33 million Pounds. On the surface this seems to be a reduction of £49000 from last year. But Allah has bestowed so much from other sources that when I added them to the Waqf e Jadid, it resulted in an increase rather than a decrease.

The shortfall which occurred was due to a reduction in the subscriptions of Waqf e Jadid in USA. This reduction took place because they followed my directives. It is a distinction of the USA Jamaat that they follow as they are directed. But in spite of the reduction there are two ways in which the USA has not fallen short. Firstly, in spite of the reduction USA is still number one in Waqf e Jadid. Pakistan is number two.

If we take the reduction in USA, the overall shortfall in Waqf e Jadid should have been £115000 Pounds; but the actual shortfall is only £49000 Pounds, because the other Jamaats have shown increases over previous years.  But the most surprising case is that of Pakistan.  Everybody knows the state of the currency in Pakistan, still Pakistan was able to increase its payments by nineteen thousand Pounds.

The number of contributors is also good news.  Last year there were 272200; this year it stands at 273300.

The UK has increased its contribution from £50000 Pounds to £100000 Pounds; although it does not change its standing in the tables, it is a considerable increase.  Moreover, the UK has offered £72000 Pounds for use for Jamaat needs.  It will be used in the African countries, where some of the Waqf e Jadid funds are spent.

The USA had reached a level of $900000 dollars, but 85% of it was contributed by 15 members who are affluent.  I have directed them to increase the contribution by common members.  Now that 90% of the members are contributing, the percentage which was being contributed by the 15 members.  This is very good news.  Pakistan is Number two, Germany number three, UK number four.  Burma has made an outstanding sacrifice.

I advise the Jamaat to eschew extravagance and spend in the way of Allah. All members in affluent countries should take up the flag of fighting extravagance. The money that is wasted in the USA alone can feed all the poor countries.

The Promised Messiah (peace be upon him) received the revelation (in Urdu): I shall carry thy message to the far ends of the world. It was first revealed in 1897, but was repeated several times. In August 1998 (the revelation was also received in August 1898) we were to reserve a new channel for transmitting MTA to Australia and New Zealand where we were unable to reach so far.

In 1899 the Promised Messiah (peace be upon him) received a revelation (in Urdu): Those who are given glad tidings [from God] suffer no decline; the time of the fulfillment of the prophecies of the Governor General has arrived. I can clearly see how these prophecies are about to be fulfilled. I shall Inshallah give more details at the Jalsa Salana. My attention was drawn to this revelation because of a dream of Masood Ahmad Sahib of Delhi. His name is also Masood which means fortunate.

A time of blessings is about to start for Ahmadiyyat. When Allah decides to grant these blessings to the Jamaat, no power on earth can stop them.
